Tests for JavaScript error objects, including Error, TypeError, ReferenceError, EvalError, RangeError, SyntaxError, and URIError.

# Purpose
This code is a test suite for JavaScript error objects. It verifies the behavior and properties of various built-in error types, including `Error`, `TypeError`, `ReferenceError`, `EvalError`, `RangeError`, `SyntaxError`, and `URIError`. The code creates instances of each error type, checks their default properties such as `name` and `message`, and confirms their string representations using the `toString` method. It also tests the prototype chain to ensure that each error type's prototype is correctly set up, including the `constructor` and `toString` methods.

The code includes assertions to validate that the error objects behave as expected. It uses `try-catch` blocks to test the instantiation and handling of `TypeError` and `ReferenceError` when specific erroneous operations are performed. The assertions confirm that the caught errors are instances of the expected error types and that they inherit from the `Error` and `Object` classes. This test suite ensures that the error handling mechanisms in JavaScript conform to the expected standards and behaviors.
